title: Existence of user storage.conf (rootless) causes driver errors
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Overriding the system-wide storage.conf is causing errors.

Without user-specific storage.conf podman works fine. As soon as I create $HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf with this content (which is identical with the system-wide settings):

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot="$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
```

any podman commands fail with this error:

```
$ podman info
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlayfs" overwritten by graph driver "overlay" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over <unknown> at "/home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

I wiped $HOME/.local completely and still the error persists.

My goal is to use an additional disk for storing containers for a particular user - I'd like to change graphRoot therefore on a user-basis.

For the storage driver I tried ```driver = "fuse-overlayfs"```as well which did not help.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create file $HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf and add this content:
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot="$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
```

2. Run "podman info"

**Describe the results you received:**

$ podman info
```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlayfs" overwritten by graph driver "overlay" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over <unknown> at "/home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```
**Describe the results you expected:**

Usual output of podman info with all settings.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman --version
podman version 3.2.3
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

same error as with "podman --version" above

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.2.3-0.11.module+el8.4.0+12050+ef972f71.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
VERSION="8.4 (Ootpa)"
ID="rhel"
ID_LIKE="fedora"
VERSION_ID="8.4"
PLATFORM_ID="platform:el8"
PRETTY_NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4 (Ootpa)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:8.4:GA"
HOME_URL="https://www.redhat.com/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://access.redhat.com/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"

REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=8.4
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="8.4"
````

```
$ uname -a
Linux xxx 4.18.0-305.19.1.el8_4.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Sep 7 07:07:31 EDT 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

```
$ rpm -q fuse-overlayfs
fuse-overlayfs-1.6-1.module+el8.4.0+11822+6cc1e7d7.x86_64
```

```
$ head -n 15 /etc/containers/storage.conf 
# This file is is the configuration file for all tools
# that use the containers/storage library.
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information
# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver, Must be set for proper operation.
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
```

Filesystem is ext4.

comments.body:
If I switch the driver to "overlay" instead of "overlayfs" in the user's storage.conf I still receive an error:

```
$ podman info
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over <unknown> at "/home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

Can you try adding:
```
[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
Your kernel does not support rootless overlayfs.

> Can you try adding:
> 
> ```
> [storage.options.overlay]
> mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
> ```
> 
> Your kernel does not support rootless overlayfs.

Wow. This helped. Thanks a lot. The error is gone and podman info outputs all settings nicely.

However I'm still puzzled because ```mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"``` is already defined in /etc/containers/storage.conf. And as I did not define mount_program in the user's storage.conf at all I would assume it should still be valid/applied.
Users storage.conf does not currently inherit from the system one, it overrides it.
We need to make this more clear - maybe a comment at the top of the system-level `storage.conf` that this is not inherited by rootless users?
Sure you have to be clear on the wording.  Open a PR in containers/storage.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

It will be awesome to inimize using Path environment variables in the storage.conf and use only configuration files merge because manipulation with environment in the systemd units is the hell: generators + Environment= + EnvironmentFile=  + PassEnvironment= + PostExec=systemctl --user --export-environment, DBUS .....
@rauar Why you didn't create symbolic link ?
